front.title: Temporal Computing: AI and Time Travel in Fiction
front.subtitle: Exploring how science fiction uses artificial intelligence to manage the paradoxes and calculations of time travel.

epochEvents.name: Temporal Computing: AI and Time Travel in Fiction

back.text:
In science fiction, time travel is often depicted as a computational challenge beyond human capability. Authors frequently employ AI as the 'navigator' for temporal shifts, using machine learning to calculate complex trajectories through 4D space-time and to predict the cascading consequences of altering the past, often referred to as the Butterfly Effect. These fictional systems serve as a metaphor for real-world predictive modeling and the ethical implications of using data to alter future outcomes.

Prominent examples include AI entities that monitor 'sacred timelines' or prevent temporal paradoxes. These stories explore the concept of 'causality loops,' where an AI might be responsible for its own creation by sending information back in time. While physical time travel remains theoretical, these narratives reflect modern concerns about algorithmic determinism and the power of AI to shape the course of human history through long-term forecasting and automated decision-making.
